items.text: She presented a {1} argument that flowed logically from start to finish.
items.explanation: Coherent means clear, logical, and well organised.

items.text: He gave an {1} description, covering every tiny detail.
items.explanation: Elaborate means detailed and carefully developed.

items.text: Within the company {1}, decisions flow from the top down.
items.explanation: A hierarchy is a system ranking people by authority.

items.text: Half of the report was {1} and could be safely removed.
items.explanation: Redundant means unnecessary because it repeats something.

items.text: A good essay should {1} ideas from several different sources.
items.explanation: To synthesise is to combine ideas into a coherent whole.

items.text: The contract was placed under intense {1} by the lawyers.
items.explanation: Scrutiny is close and critical examination.

items.text: He {1} deleted the file while trying to organise his folders.
items.explanation: Inadvertently means accidentally, without intending to.

items.text: The watchmaker was known for her {1} attention to detail.
items.explanation: Meticulous means showing great care and precision.

items.text: A skilled translator captures every {1} of the original text.
items.explanation: A nuance is a subtle difference in meaning or tone.

items.text: Her alibi sounded entirely {1} to the detectives.
items.explanation: Plausible means seeming reasonable or probably true.

items.text: The negotiations reached a {1} that neither side could break.
items.explanation: A stalemate is a situation where no progress is possible.

items.text: His {1} remarks offended several people in the audience.
items.explanation: Derogatory means showing a critical or disrespectful attitude.

items.text: The evidence was merely {1} and proved nothing for certain.
items.explanation: Circumstantial evidence suggests but does not directly prove a fact.

items.text: The policy had a {1} effect on the entire economy.
items.explanation: Profound means very great or deeply felt.

items.text: The two accounts of the event were difficult to {1}.
items.explanation: To reconcile two things is to make them consistent.

items.text: Her success was {1} on years of quiet, persistent effort.
items.explanation: To be predicated on something means to depend on it.

items.text: The professor's lecture was dense and at times {1}.
items.explanation: Impenetrable here means impossible to understand.

items.text: They reached a {1} agreement after months of dispute.
items.explanation: Tentative means not certain or final, provisional.

items.text: The witness's story was full of {1} that undermined her credibility.
items.explanation: A discrepancy is a difference between things that should match.

items.text: The author writes with a {1} that few can match.
items.explanation: Eloquence is fluent and persuasive expression.

items.text: His {1} comments hinted at a deeper disagreement.
items.explanation: Cryptic means mysterious and hard to interpret.

items.text: The committee remained {1} despite enormous public pressure.
items.explanation: Resolute means firmly determined and unwavering.

items.text: The two theories are not mutually {1}; both can be true.
items.explanation: Mutually exclusive things cannot both be true at once.

items.text: The new evidence served only to {1} the controversy.
items.explanation: To exacerbate is to make a problem worse.

items.text: Her argument rested on a {1} assumption that everyone agreed.
items.explanation: Spurious means false or not based on sound reasoning.

items.text: The manager has the {1} to approve large expenses.
items.explanation: A prerogative is a special right or privilege.

items.text: His expertise in the field is {1}; no one questions it.
items.explanation: Indisputable means impossible to doubt or argue against.

items.text: The report tried to {1} a complex issue into simple terms.
items.explanation: To distil is to extract the essential meaning of something.

items.text: Her tone was {1}, perfectly matching the formal occasion.
items.explanation: Measured here means careful and deliberately controlled.

items.text: The findings {1} the long-held belief about the brain.
items.explanation: To corroborate is to confirm or support with evidence.

items.text: His apology felt {1} rather than genuinely heartfelt.
items.explanation: Perfunctory means done without real interest or care.

items.text: The novel offers a {1} critique of modern society.
items.explanation: Scathing means harshly and severely critical.

items.text: The two sides finally reached a workable {1}.
items.explanation: A compromise is an agreement reached by mutual concessions.

items.text: Such a bold claim demands {1} supporting evidence.
items.explanation: Compelling means convincing and impossible to ignore.
